behavior_summary: U8P_NSRUNWOD::look is a simple U8P_NSRUNWOD::look/examine handler that displays a single short bark text "wood " when the player inspects the object/NPC.
After emitting the bark the process appears to yield or terminate (a suspend token is present), so no further logic, branching, or side effects are performed.
No global flags, parameters, locals, or external calls are used by this script; behavior is static and purely presentational.
notable_control_flow: Entry on a U8P_NSRUNWOD::look/examine trigger leads directly to emitting a bark and then suspending/ending the process.
There are no conditional branches, menu interactions, or external calls—control flow is linear and ends immediately after the bark.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark string "wood " intentional (formatting for concatenation) or an artifact of extraction? (low confidence)
Does the observed 'suspend' represent an immediate process termination or a yield allowing other engine processing (medium confidence)?

main_function.name: U8P_NSRUNWOD::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines or 'looks at' this object/NPC by emitting a single bark 'wood '.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player performs a U8P_NSRUNWOD::look/examine action on the associated object/NPC
Also could be triggered by engine fast-area/U8P_NSRUNWOD::look-type events that route to the object's U8P_NSRUNWOD::look process
